covered_sentences: The next day I taught Friday how to make bread.
After a little time he was able to do these things as well as I could do them myself.
This was the best year of my life on the island.
Now I had someone to talk to, and he was a pleasant fellow.

Left panel: camera positioned slightly above and behind the island-dweller's right shoulder (over-shoulder view), angled left toward the companion at the camp cooking area; foreground shows the island-dweller's right shoulder and arm, mid-ground shows the companion kneading dough on a flat wooden board. Right panel: camera at mid-height, angled slightly right 20°, framing both men in medium shot sitting together near the palisade interior, companion gesturing animatedly, island-dweller smiling back; background shows the earth cliff wall.

Left panel: island-dweller's right shoulder and arm occupy the left foreground (~1/4 of panel width), right hand pointing toward the dough board; companion stands at a low wooden table, both hands pressing into a round lump of dough on a flat board, glancing back at the island-dweller with a focused expression, a thin wisp of grain dust visible near the board. Right panel: island-dweller sits on a low stool near the palisade wall, left arm resting on knee, smiling warmly; companion sits opposite cross-legged, gesturing with both hands as if demonstrating something, face bright and enthusiastic. Small cloud-thought bubble from island-dweller: simple calendar marks (tally lines suggesting a year) + a small smiling face sketch.

covered_sentences: One day I talked to Friday about his nation.
I asked him what his nation did with the prisoners they took in battle.
Do they carry them away and eat them as these did I asked.
My nation is the man too.

covered_sentences: Do they ever carry prisoners to this island yes, sometimes have you been here with them Friday yes, I have been here he pointed to the western side.
So Friday had been one of the cannibals who used to come to the other side of the island.
He told me that one time he had eaten twenty men, two women and a child.
